seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service ai chatbot

content.intro: Building AI Chatbots with Azure AI Agent Service enables enterprise-grade conversational agents powered by Azure OpenAI, retrieval-augmented generation, and built-in grounding capabilities. However, without proper guardrails, these agents risk policy violations, hallucinations, PII leaks, and token exhaustion in multi-turn conversations. Vex provides essential middleware to validate tool outputs, ensure response safety, and maintain conversation coherence at scale.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
Azure AI Agent Service orchestrates conversational agents using Azure OpenAI models with RAG capabilities via Bing Search and file search connectors. User queries trigger intent classification, followed by tool execution (search, function calling) and multi-turn thread management. Responses are generated with grounding data and enterprise security controls applied.

The pipeline processes messages through agent threads, maintaining conversation history while respecting token limits. Grounding sources include enterprise knowledge bases and real-time Bing search, with outputs validated against Azure policies. Multi-turn memory uses thread state to preserve context across interactions.

Vex integrates as middleware between agent execution and response delivery, validating tool results, checking safety, and ensuring coherence before user delivery.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Bing Search or file search returns data violating DLP policies or containing restricted content
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware scans all tool outputs against enterprise PII/safety policies before agent synthesis

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Real-time search returns stale or incorrect data not verified against knowledge freshness policies
risks.mitigation: Vex validates search result recency and cross-references with enterprise knowledge cutoffs

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: RAG returns file metadata/permissions instead of semantic content, causing hallucinated responses
risks.mitigation: Vex extracts and validates actual content from file search results before RAG processing

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Multi-turn conversations exceed context windows without summarization or thread reset
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers conversation summarization or thread archival

implementation.title: Initialize Azure AI Agent Client with Thread Management
implementation.description: Create agent threads for multi-turn conversations with proper token limit handling
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectsClient
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
client = AIProjectsClient(
    endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/",
    credential=credential,
    api_version="2024-10-21"
)

# Create or get agent thread
thread = client.agents.create_thread(
    agent_id="your-chatbot-agent-id",
    message="Welcome to enterprise chatbot"
)
print(f"Thread created: {thread.id}")

implementation.title: Process User Message with RAG Grounding
implementation.description: Send user query through agent with Bing search and file search grounding enabled
implementation.code:
async def process_message(thread_id: str, user_message: str):
    from azure.ai.projects.aio import AIProjectsClient
    
    client = AIProjectsClient(
        endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/",
        credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
        api_version="2024-10-21"
    )
    
    # Add user message to thread
    await client.agents.create_message(
        agent_id="your-chatbot-agent-id",
        thread_id=thread_id,
        role="user",
        content=[{"type": "text", "text": user_message}]
    )
    
    # Run agent with grounding
    run = await client.agents.create_run(
        agent_id="your-chatbot-agent-id",
        thread_id=thread_id,
        assistant_config={
            "tools": [{"type": "bing_search"}, {"type": "file_search"}],
            "model": "gpt-4o",
            "instructions": "You are a helpful enterprise assistant"
        }
    )
    return run.id

vexIntegration.title: Vex Middleware for Tool Result Validation
vexIntegration.description: Intercept agent tool calls and validate Bing/file search outputs against policies

vexIntegration.title: Response Safety and Hallucination Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Final response validation for accuracy, safety, and coherence before delivery
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

def validate_final_response(agent_response: str, conversation_history: list) -> dict:
    verification = vex.verify_response(
        response=agent_response,
        conversation=conversation_history[-5:],  # Last 5 turns
        checks=[
            "response_accuracy",
            "conversation_coherence",
            "safety_filtering",
            "pii_protection",
            "hallucination_detection"
        ],
        risk_profile="high",
        model="gpt-4o"
    )
    
    if verification.score < 0.85:
        # Auto-trigger response refinement
        refined = vex.refine_response(
            original=agent_response,
            issues=verification.issues,
            conversation=conversation_history
        )
        return {"approved": False, "refined": refined}
    
    return {"approved": True, "score": verification.score}
